chapters.content_preview: 2. Works of Ganesa Daivajiia  Ganega composed several important works on astronomy, as- trology etc among which his astronomical treatise Grahaldghavam is the most famous. In fact, the remarkable popularity of the Graha ## Page 3  INTRODUCTION Gi)

chapters.title: Special features of Grahalaghavam
chapters.content_preview: 3. Special features of Grahalaghavam  Ganeéga has simplified the method of computations of the po- sitions of planets which is otherwise laborious by the traditional method.  To avoid a huge number for the ahargana (number of civil days since the epoch), GaneSa has adopted an aha

chapters.title: Popularity of Grahalaghavam and its commentators
chapters.content_preview: 4. Popularity of Grahalaghavam and its commentators  As mentioned earlier, the Grahalaghavam of Ganega Daivajfia is the most popular astronomical text, among the ancient and medi- eval texts, currently used in most parts of India. Further, among the karana works (hand-books) on I

chapters.title: Some special results of Ganega Daivajiia
chapters.content_preview: 5. Some special results of Ganega Daivajiia (i) Phase of the Moon  From the newmoon to the fullmoon, the phase of the Moon increases, given by  phase = (1+ cos d)/2

chapters.title: Grahalaghavam (GL)
chapters.content_preview: 6. Grahalaghavam (GL)  In our present work, for each Sloka we have given a working English translation along with mathematical explanation, deriva- tions, worked examples, tables and diagrams wherever necessary. This work is based on the printed edition of GL, with commentaries #

chapters.title: Introduction
chapters.content_preview: 2.1. Introduction  In obtaining the mean positions of the Sun and the Moon, it was assumed that these bodies move in circular orbits round the earth with uniform angular velocities. However, by observations it was found that  the motions are non-uniform.

chapters.title: True daily motion of Kuja
chapters.content_preview: 1. True daily motion of Kuja : (i) Obtain the Manda-corrected motion as explained in the previous Sloka.  (ii) Take the difference between Sighranikas which are obtained in find- ing the second Sighraphala. ## Page 136  $124 GRAHALAGHAVAM OF GANESA DAIVAJNA

chapters.title: True daily motion of Budha
chapters.content_preview: 2. True daily motion of Budha (i) Obtain the Manda corrected Budha’s motion as explained earlier. chapters.title: True Motion of Guru
chapters.content_preview: 8. True Motion of Guru :  (i) Obtain the Manda corrected motion of Guru as explained earlier.  (ii) Obtain the difference of Sighrarikas which are obtained in finding the second Sighraphala. ## Page 138

chapters.title: True motion of Sukra
chapters.content_preview: 4. True motion of Sukra :  (i) Obtain the Manda corrected motion of Sukra as explained in the pre- vious Sloka.  (ii) Obtain the difference between the Sighrankas of the second Sighraphala.

chapters.title: True daily motion of Sani
chapters.content_preview: 5. True daily motion of Sani : (i) Obtain the Manda-corrected motion of Sani as explained earlier. chapters.title: When the Sun and lagna are in the same rasi, consider the difference
chapters.content_preview: 1. When the Sun and lagna are in the same rasi, consider the difference between the Sun and the lagna.  2. Multiply the above difference by the duration of rising of the raéi in which both the Sun and the lagna are present.  3. Divide the product obtained in step (2) by 30. This

chapters.title: Multiply the above difference by the duration of rising of the raéi in
chapters.content_preview: 2. Multiply the above difference by the duration of rising of the raéi in which both the Sun and the lagna are present.  3. Divide the product obtained in step (2) by 30. This gives the time in palas at the given lagna. Divide it by 60 to convert it into ghatis.  Note : If lagna

chapters.title: Divide the product obtained in step (2) by 30. This gives the time in
chapters.content_preview: 3. Divide the product obtained in step (2) by 30. This gives the time in palas at the given lagna. Divide it by 60 to convert it into ghatis.  Note : If lagna < the Sun, then subtract from 60 the time obtained in step (3).  Example 1 : Séyana lagna = 1" 98° 37'50" Sayana Ravi = 1
